user_query:
I'm putting together a sibling-friendly explainer on a few TLS cipher suites I keep mentioning in my stories. Please use the local inputs and an official registry to build a clean, verifiable table and a short message for my younger sibling.

Inputs and configuration:
- Input CSV: input/tls_suites.csv (columns: hex_value,note)
- Config file to be modified: config/filters.yaml

What to do:
1) Download the official IANA "TLS Cipher Suites" registry in CSV form from the IANA TLS Parameters registries on the iana.org domain. Save the raw file to downloads/iana_tls_cipher_suites.csv. Do not use a mirror or a third-party site.
2) Extract structured data from the downloaded CSV. For each hex_value in input/tls_suites.csv, join on the IANA registry's Value field (hex-insensitive; treat 0x9c and 0x009C as the same). From the registry, use at least the following columns if present: Description and Recommended (case-insensitive header matching is fine). Create an enriched table with these columns in this order:
   - hex_value (from input)
   - iana_description (from Description)
   - iana_recommended (from Recommended)
   - note (from input)
   - normalized_name (a lowercase, hyphen-separated version of iana_description, only ASCII letters, numbers, and hyphens)
   Save this to out/tls_suites_enriched.csv with a header.
3) Modify config/filters.yaml as follows:
   - Set sibling_name to "Riley" (replacing the placeholder).
   - Set output_fields to exactly ["hex_value", "iana_description", "iana_recommended", "note"].
   Leave include_recommended_only: true as-is and keep highlight_keywords unchanged.
4) Using the modified config, produce a filtered CSV out/tls_suites_recommended.csv that includes only those rows from out/tls_suites_enriched.csv where iana_recommended indicates the suite is recommended by IANA (treat typical yes/no values case-insensitively, e.g., "Y", "Yes"). The columns and their order must match output_fields from the config.
5) Draft a short plaintext message to my sibling using sibling_name from the config. Save it to out/email_to_sibling.txt. The message should:
   - Start with a greeting that includes the sibling's name from the config.
   - In 3–6 sentences, explain what the table represents and why some suites are recommended.
   - Include a simple bullet list (one line per item) of the recommended suites found, showing hex_value and a simplified name (use normalized_name or iana_description) for each.
   - Reference the two CSV output paths so they know where to look.

Output paths to create:
- downloads/iana_tls_cipher_suites.csv (downloaded registry)
- out/tls_suites_enriched.csv (joined and enriched table)
- out/tls_suites_recommended.csv (filtered, columns per config)
- out/email_to_sibling.txt (drafted message)

Key constraints:
- The join must be robust to hex formatting differences (case and leading zeros) when matching values.
- Do not hardcode registry values; parse them from the downloaded CSV.
- out/tls_suites_recommended.csv must include exactly the columns specified by output_fields in the modified config, in that order.
- Use only the IANA registry specified above for authoritative names/status, and keep all processing local after download. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
